summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on zoning case ZC 2025-006, a preliminary development plan for the Eagle's Nest Golf Course site submitted by Drees Homes. The proposal seeks to rezone 163.38 acres from PUD/PDBB to Planned Development Mixed Use (PDM), encompassing 177 single-family homes, 126 townhomes, 252 apartments, and approximately 28 acres of commercial space including a grocery store, gas station, and retail locations. After staff presentation, applicant testimony, and limited public comment, the Commission voted unanimously to recommend approval with 10+ stipulations including a traffic impact study, phased development schedule, and minimum square footage requirements.
decisions: Approved minutes from July 1, 2025 meeting (motion by Lewis, second by Curry)
Unanimously recommended approval of zoning case ZC 2025-006 rezoning parcel 112203B047 from PUD/PDBB to PDM with stipulations (motion by Pegram, second by Dinwiddie, 6-0)
October Zoning Commission meeting canceled due to no pending applications
action_items: Developer (Drees Homes) to complete ODOT traffic impact study covering State Route 28, Smith Road, Fay Road, and State Route 48 and implement warranted items
Developer to provide a phased development schedule with commercial properties as priority and 50% phase completion requirement before proceeding to next phase
Developer to provide elevation drawings for businesses to confirm conformance with Goshen Township architectural standards
Developer to provide floor plans showing apartments meet 700/750 sq ft minimum and single-family homes meet 1,400 sq ft minimum
Developer to provide drawings showing pedestrian connectivity between residential and commercial areas
Developer to consider a development agreement with Goshen Township addressing flock cameras, existing TIF, infrastructure financing, and road improvements
Developer to continue conversations with Cincinnati United Soccer Club about interconnection and traffic relief on State Route 28
No streetscape to be placed between curb and sidewalks per Service Department requirement
Temporary and permanent signage to include Goshen Township where applicable
Street signs must match Goshen Township color and style standards
Zoning Commission, BZA, and Trustees to attend special joint workshop meeting on Monday September 8, 2025 at 6 PM regarding zoning code revisions
key_amounts: 252 apartments (21 acres at approximately 12 units per acre)
54 townhome units (18 three-unit buildings) plus 126 townhomes total mentioned
555 total residential units referenced from earlier plan
Apartment rents expected $1,200 to $1,900 per month
Apartment sizes expected 750 to 1,400 sq ft
1,400 sq ft minimum for single-family dwellings
700-750 sq ft minimum for apartment units
50-foot buffer required adjacent to residential properties
50 fewer detached lots and 50 fewer apartments than originally proposed
notable_topics: Rezoning of Eagle's Nest Golf Course from PUD/PDBB to PDM (Planned Development Mixed Use)
Preliminary development plan review for Drees Homes mixed-use community
Traffic impact study requirements from Clermont County Engineer and ODOT
Police Department request for flock safety cameras at all ingress/egress points
Buffering and screening requirements for adjacent residential properties
Phased development schedule and commercial priority
HOA structure including master HOA and residential HOA
Cincinnati United Soccer Club facility interconnection
Goshen Township zoning code revision process ongoing
Service Department requests for centralized mailboxes and no streetscape between curb and sidewalks
Development agreement considerations including TIF and infrastructure
attendees: Mr. Otte (Zoning Commission member - roll call only)
notes: The transcript contains numerous repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.' artifacts that appear to be automated captioning errors or overlay text, significantly degrading portions of the transcript. Mr. Hodges was noted as not present during minutes vote. Mr. Otte was present for roll call but not mentioned during votes. The original PUD zoning was approved in 2005 and has undergone multiple modifications including a minor change in April 2024. The chair emphasized that the decision to develop the golf course was made in 2005 and the Commission's role is to evaluate whether the plan meets zoning code requirements. A special joint workshop for zoning code revisions is scheduled for September 8, 2025, which is not open to public comment.
title: ZC 9-2-25
